jokes.text: I hate it when they hire Yanks to play Brits. You can always tell.
jokes.setup: Rita, who is British, complains about Americans playing British characters poorly
jokes.punchline: The irony that she herself is likely not who she appears to be
jokes.explanation: Early foreshadowing with perfect irony - Rita complaining about fake accents while being revealed later as not who she seems

jokes.text: Besides, I already got him his big present for his birthday. A suit! Dad, it's a Jack Welch. I want you to look under the pants.
jokes.setup: Michael proudly announces he got George Michael a suit as a birthday present
jokes.punchline: The awkward phrasing 'look under the pants' and giving a business suit to a teenager
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael trying to be a good dad but completely missing what a teenager would want

jokes.text: Quicken! Premier. Dad, I hope you kept the receipt. - You wanna return that? - No, I wanna deduct it.
jokes.setup: Michael discovers the suit comes with business software, mentions keeping receipt
jokes.punchline: His immediate thought is tax deduction rather than returning inappropriate gift
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael character moment - turning even gift-giving into business

jokes.text: Really, he would only like it for the receipt.
jokes.setup: Discussion about getting George Michael a train
jokes.punchline: Michael's cynical but accurate assessment of his business-minded son
jokes.explanation: Sad but funny truth about how George Michael has absorbed the family's business obsession

jokes.text: Okay, that's, uh, that wa... that was fun. Still, the land probably shouldn't have given out beneath us.
jokes.setup: Michael and Rita apparently just fell through unstable ground
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact understatement about a construction disaster
jokes.explanation: Classic deadpan delivery treating a serious construction failure as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: I won't tattle.
jokes.setup: Michael worried about Japanese investors finding out about construction problems
jokes.punchline: Rita's childish word choice 'tattle' in adult business context
jokes.explanation: Another hint at Rita's true nature through inappropriate childish language

jokes.text: - Whee! - And Rita was falling for Michael too. But that wasn't part of the plan.
jokes.setup: Rita literally falling down the hill
jokes.punchline: Narrator's pun on 'falling' both physically and romantically, plus hint of conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Clever visual/verbal pun that also advances the spy plot - classic AD layered humor

jokes.text: I'm a woman too! Not some robot you get to tell everything to.
jokes.setup: Rita arguing with her handler about falling in love with Michael
jokes.punchline: Childish tantrum language mixed with spy drama
jokes.explanation: Rita's emotional outburst reveals both spy plot and her mental limitations

jokes.text: 'You do the math, Rita.' Well, no, you do the math.
jokes.setup: Rita's angry rant about her handler's instructions
jokes.punchline: Childish 'no you' comeback in spy context
jokes.explanation: Perfect childish response that hints at Rita's true mental state

jokes.text: I'm a big girl.
jokes.setup: Handler asking if Rita can handle removing Michael from the picture
jokes.punchline: Childish phrase used to assert adult capability
jokes.explanation: Another perfectly childish phrase that undermines her supposed spy competence

jokes.text: - Hey, what happened to you? - Oh, I got highlights. - It'll blend in about a week.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what happened, expecting answer about construction collapse
jokes.punchline: Gob thinks he's asking about his hair highlights
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection gag playing on Gob's vanity and obliviousness

jokes.text: It probably has something to do with the 4,000 hair plugs I had in one sitting.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Gob's appearance issues
jokes.punchline: Absurdly excessive number of hair plugs in one session
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob excess and vanity taken to absurd extremes

jokes.text: Yeah, but he's too busy going to the gym to see his friend Frank. Oh, we're more than friends. I'm kind of his spotter. Missed a spot.
jokes.setup: Gob talking about his gym relationship with Frank
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'spotter' (gym term) and 'missed a spot' (hair plugs)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting gym terminology to his hair transplant issues

jokes.text: - You're blowing my mind, Frank. - I'm an agent, and I'd like to work with you. Oh. Oh!
jokes.setup: Gob thinks Frank is making romantic advances
jokes.punchline: Frank reveals he's a talent agent, deflating Gob's assumption
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on Gob's narcissism and the double meaning setup

jokes.text: Frank said CIA, a government agency that was responsible for this catastrophe. But Tobias thought he said CAA, a talent agency that was responsible for this one.
jokes.setup: Narrator explains the miscommunication between Frank and Tobias
jokes.punchline: Visual joke showing terrible movie poster, implying CAA's responsibility for bad entertainment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dual-catastrophe joke connecting spy agencies and Hollywood agencies as equally destructive

jokes.text: Actually, she'd already gotten one... as the movie she'd released in her secret life as a studio executive was losing money.
jokes.setup: Michael worried Maeby will start getting F's
jokes.punchline: She already got an F - her movie flopped as a studio executive
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback connecting school grades to her secret Hollywood career

jokes.text: - But didn't some people die in Hell Tunnel? - That is an urban legend. Two elderly people got badly scalded...
jokes.setup: Maeby dismisses deaths as urban legend
jokes.punchline: Immediately describes serious injuries in detail while maintaining it's just a legend
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction joke - denying deaths while describing serious injuries

jokes.text: - You know, she was in the Olympics? - She wasn't. - Wasn't she on the cover of Newsweek? - No. - Yes. - Michael was thinking of this man.
jokes.setup: Michael insisting Rita was on Newsweek cover
jokes.punchline: Visual of male athlete, showing Michael's complete confusion
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag showing how completely wrong Michael is about Rita's supposed achievements

jokes.text: The whole backside has moles. That's Frank's problem too.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions property has mole problem
jokes.punchline: Gob connects it to Frank's physical moles
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun connecting two different meanings of 'mole'

jokes.text: Hey, I didn't say that.
jokes.setup: George Sr. (through Larry) saying the surrogate is worth every penny
jokes.punchline: Larry breaking character to deny saying something positive about himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the absurdity of the surrogate system and Larry's limitations.

jokes.text: - Typical. - Delicious.
jokes.setup: Lucille's disgusted 'typical' response to Michael's British girlfriend comment
jokes.punchline: Gob misunderstands and thinks she's talking about food
jokes.explanation: Quick misdirection gag showing Gob's obliviousness to context

jokes.text: - I need a drink. - I'll take an English muffin.
jokes.setup: Lucille asking for alcohol
jokes.punchline: Gob treating it like a restaurant order, connecting to British theme
jokes.explanation: Another quick Gob misunderstanding, this time connecting British girlfriend to British food

jokes.text: And Godzilla! - Godzirra! - Just sit down. - Okay, guys. - Godzirra!
jokes.setup: Discussion of respecting Japanese culture
jokes.punchline: Family immediately doing racist Godzilla impressions
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor showing family's immediate cultural insensitivity despite good intentions

jokes.text: - What? We're out of vodka. - Sorry. I thought you were doing Godzilla.
jokes.setup: Lucille makes monster noise
jokes.punchline: She's actually just expressing alcohol withdrawal, not doing impression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - her alcoholism mistaken for racist impression

jokes.text: Build a miniature city. Put it outside their window. Tell them it's far away. It'll look real if you squint. God knows they're squinters.
jokes.setup: Gob suggests building miniature city to fool Japanese investors
jokes.punchline: Racist assumption about Asian people squinting will make deception work
jokes.explanation: Absurd plan made worse by casual racism, typical Gob obliviousness

jokes.text: - Another brilliant idea, Einstein. - Really? - Larry really never knows how to sell the sarcasm.
jokes.setup: George Sr. sarcastically calls Gob 'Einstein'
jokes.punchline: Gob takes it as genuine compliment, narrator explains Larry's poor sarcasm delivery
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about the surrogate's inability to convey tone, plus Gob's obliviousness

jokes.text: Oh, great. That's another brilliant idea, Steinberg.
jokes.setup: George Sr. being sarcastic again
jokes.punchline: Accidentally uses Jewish surname instead of Einstein, revealing his casual antisemitism
jokes.explanation: Reveals George Sr.'s prejudices through his confused attempt at sarcasm

jokes.text: Life is not some cheesy Japanese movie... where the hero pulls on a pair of jet pants and... flies off the balcony like Astroboy.
jokes.setup: George Sr. dismissing unrealistic Japanese movie scenarios
jokes.punchline: Specific description of jet pants that will literally happen later
jokes.explanation: Perfect foreshadowing disguised as dismissive comment about unrealistic scenarios

jokes.text: And that's when George Sr. Did a Web search for the words, 'Jet pants.'
jokes.setup: George Sr. just dismissed jet pants as unrealistic
jokes.punchline: Immediately searches for them online
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback showing George Sr.'s hypocrisy and desperation

jokes.text: Let's start with my family's misdemeanors... and then push right on through to the lighter treasons.
jokes.setup: Michael casually discussing his family's crimes with Rita
jokes.punchline: 'Lighter treasons' as if treason comes in different weights
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation joke treating serious crimes casually, with perfect word choice 'lighter'

jokes.text: Do you know any other songs?
jokes.setup: Michael has been sharing family crimes while Rita plays banjo
jokes.punchline: His casual request for different music during confession of serious crimes
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - treating serious confession like dinner entertainment

jokes.text: - And it could be an alias. - Alias is a show about a spy!
jokes.setup: Discussion of 'Mr. F' possibly being an alias
jokes.punchline: Michael's connection to the TV show Alias
jokes.explanation: Meta reference to actual TV show fits naturally into spy plot discussion

jokes.text: Hell Tunnel takes another name. Will it take another life?
jokes.setup: TV promo for dangerous studio attraction
jokes.punchline: Ominous pun on 'taking' a name vs taking a life
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating deadly attraction as entertainment, with clever wordplay

jokes.text: I'd wear it to school, but who needs a Welch wedge, right? That's when they... Enough business.
jokes.setup: George Michael about to explain financial terminology
jokes.punchline: Michael cutting him off before he gets too deep into business talk
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how business-obsessed George Michael has become.

jokes.text: 'Fun' and 'Failure' both start out the same way.
jokes.setup: Michael encouraging George Michael to have fun
jokes.punchline: George Michael's pessimistic wordplay observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael line - innocent observation revealing deep pessimism and Bluth family damage

jokes.text: - I'm never gonna find my way... - past that hallway sensor.
jokes.setup: Larry expressing frustration
jokes.punchline: George Sr. finishing his sentence about the security system
jokes.explanation: Another example of Larry's inability to sync with George Sr.'s intended words

jokes.text: If mother sees this, she will blow a cow.
jokes.setup: Buster worried about Lucille's reaction
jokes.punchline: Mixes up 'blow a gasket' and 'have a cow'
jokes.explanation: Classic Buster malapropism combining two common expressions incorrectly

jokes.text: Goody! Yeah. I do want to. Yea. I do want to. Goody.
jokes.setup: Buster excited about building model town with Gob
jokes.punchline: Childish enthusiasm with repeated awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster moment showing his childlike excitement and awkward speech patterns

jokes.text: Only Larry was disappointed. But he was such a pro you'd never know it.
jokes.setup: Narrator praising Larry's professionalism
jokes.punchline: Visual presumably shows Larry's obvious disappointment despite claim
jokes.explanation: Sarcastic narrator comment about Larry's obvious lack of professionalism

jokes.text: I shall be a bigger, hairier mole than the one on your inner left thigh.
jokes.setup: Frank asking Tobias to be a mole (spy)
jokes.punchline: Tobias's overly theatrical commitment and specific mole reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias over-the-top commitment with callback to earlier mole reference

jokes.text: !@%$# Tantamount.
jokes.setup: Actor frustrated about studio prioritizing tour over filming
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to curse the studio by name
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about Hollywood business priorities interrupting creative work

jokes.text: - Well, what if I crash it? - That's half the fun.
jokes.setup: George Michael worried about crashing what he thinks is a train set
jokes.punchline: Michael encouraging dangerous behavior not knowing it's a jet pack
jokes.explanation: Dark dramatic irony - Michael unwittingly encouraging son to risk his life

jokes.text: Oh! Me!@%s are wet.
jokes.setup: Dramatic confrontation in tunnel ride
jokes.punchline: Rita's childish complaint about water instead of addressing serious situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Rita moment - childish priorities during dramatic spy confrontation

jokes.text: Well, that was a freebie.
jokes.setup: Michael just gave impassioned speech about being fooled by women
jokes.punchline: Maeby's casual response treating his emotional moment as throwaway
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response deflating Michael's dramatic moment with business casual terminology

jokes.text: The jet pack... had come with an instructional DVD that seemed to confuse more than clarify.
jokes.setup: George Michael trying to understand the jet pack
jokes.punchline: Narrator's observation about unhelpful instructions
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about confusing product instructions. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: And it did seem like a lot of work... for a little payoff. - Have fun! - Have fun.
jokes.setup: George Michael struggling with jet pack instructions
jokes.punchline: Narrator's commentary applies to both jet pack and the show itself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary that works on multiple levels - jet pack instructions and show's complexity

jokes.text: And it did seem like a lot of work... for a little payoff. Have fun! Have fun.
jokes.setup: Narrator noting the work-to-payoff ratio of the jet pack
jokes.punchline: DVD's enthusiastic encouragement contrasting with the reality
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between marketing promises and actual experience.

jokes.text: We're like a couple of girls on the last day of camp.
jokes.setup: Gob and Buster having emotional moment building model town
jokes.punchline: Gob's surprisingly tender and specific comparison
jokes.explanation: Unexpected emotional vulnerability from Gob with oddly specific feminine comparison

jokes.text: He had, in the poorly narrated reality series, Scandalmakers.
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining Tobias has played George Sr. before
jokes.punchline: Self-aware dig at show's own poor quality
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating meta-humor about show's own production quality

jokes.text: Ishiro said, 'I am reassured. And they treat us with respect this time.'
jokes.setup: Japanese investors seeing the fake model city
jokes.punchline: Ironic comment about respect after all the racist behavior
jokes.explanation: Ironic that they feel respected after all the Godzilla impressions and squinting comments

jokes.text: Motoyoshi said, 'I thought the woman was pretending as a monster... but she was just out of vodka.'
jokes.setup: Japanese investor's observation about Lucille
jokes.punchline: Connecting her earlier monster noise to alcohol withdrawal rather than racism
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to earlier gag, showing outsider's perspective on Lucille's alcoholism

jokes.text: - I'm a mole! - Not the village!
jokes.setup: Tobias appearing in mole costume during presentation
jokes.punchline: Accidentally destroying the model village they just built
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation where multiple plot threads collide destructively

jokes.text: Hard to breathe in this thing. It's really hot. I'm sweating all over myself. I can't stop this thing!
jokes.setup: George Michael flying in jet pack
jokes.punchline: Panicked complaints while supposedly having 'fun'
jokes.explanation: Payoff to jet pack setup with George Michael's typical anxiety in extreme situation

jokes.text: - You're blowing my audition! - I ache with embarrassment.
jokes.setup: Chaos destroying the presentation
jokes.punchline: Tobias and Gob's theatrical responses to disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific responses to disaster - Tobias theatrical, Gob dramatic

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Tobias Fünke. You're Mr. F.
jokes.setup: Mystery of who Mr. F is throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Tobias Fünke's initials spell out the spy code name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant revelation that character's name was the answer all along - perfect AD misdirection

jokes.text: No, I'm not, Mother. I mean lover. - I like making love to Mother. - I mean lover!
jokes.setup: Buster accidentally calling Gob 'Mother'
jokes.punchline: Making it infinitely worse with 'making love to Mother' clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation where Buster's attempt to clarify makes everything infinitely worse

jokes.text: It took some idiot parrot man with a camera in his hat to bring us together.
jokes.setup: Gob reflecting on bonding with Buster
jokes.punchline: Insults Larry while acknowledging the positive outcome
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob - can't express gratitude without also being insulting

jokes.text: But even a pro like Larry has a breaking point.
jokes.setup: Continued mockery of Larry's 'professional' acting
jokes.punchline: Larry finally breaking character and leaving
jokes.explanation: Satisfying conclusion to the Larry subplot, good payoff to running gag.

jokes.text: - MRF? What's that? - Mentally retarded female.
jokes.setup: CIA agents discussing Rita's classification
jokes.punchline: Reveals Rita's true condition, recontextualizing entire relationship
jokes.explanation: Massive revelation that recontextualizes Rita's entire character and all her childish behavior

jokes.text: - That explains what she's doing with him. - And the hats.
jokes.setup: Agents explaining Rita's condition
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of her hat collection as evidence of mental disability
jokes.explanation: Retroactively makes innocent detail sinister - her hats were indicators of her condition

jokes.text: - What's that? - A prize. A lovely, lovely prize.
jokes.setup: Michael and Rita starting new life together
jokes.punchline: Rita's childish excitement about marriage certificate as 'prize'
jokes.explanation: Dark irony as Rita treats serious legal document like game show prize, given her condition
